clean_body: | = Hey team, = But before I do, let me explain just why this deadline is so important. At the end of every financial quarter, campaigns like ours have to submit all of our fundraising information to the Federal Elections Commission (FEC), which will then go public for anyone to see. Some of the key metrics from this report include the total amount raised, total number of grassroots donors, and our average contribution (which, right now, is about $20). Why do those numbers matter? Because they show the people-power behind our campaign. I ran for Congress and won because of grassroots supporters like you instead of dark money from Super PACs. The amount we raise will also determine how much we can start spending on advertising, mailers, and events as we prepare for the most competitive 2024 House election in the country. I was just named a DCCC Frontline Member and an NRCC Top-Target this month, so we need to build our resources ASAP to defend this seat from the far-right and flip the House blue. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = I’m so grateful for your continued support as I fight for NM-02 in Congress, Gabe | Gabe Vasquez is a first-generation American, former City Councilor, and conservation champion who flipped New Mexico’s 2nd Congressional District from red to blue in 2022 with the help of this people-powered movement. It’s critical that I ask you again today, Peter. = Peter, I need you to understand: = Kevin McCarthy secretly handed over security footage from January 6th to Tucker Carlson. = Donald Trump announced he will be arrested, and called on his followers to "protest" and "take our nation back." = We have a solemn obligation to protect our democracy and ensure these extreme Trump Republicans NEVER hold power again. = | | **= = = = = =** Thank you so much, Raja | Raja represents Illinois in Congress. The son of Indian immigrants, Raja grew up for a time on food stamps and in public housing. He knows the struggles countless Americans face every day. Now, Raja is leading the fight in Congress on prescription drugs, climate change, election reform, and a more inclusive economy. But Republican special interests are funnelling cash to defeat strong Democrats like Raja. He's counting on grassroots supporters to keep this campaign running strong. body:
| | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62479619:6Fb8V9GNO:m:1:3331421248:489951B05375DCE41F24010A5421B0DB:r)

Fellow Patriot, I have breaking news to share with you... My team at America First Legal just released our second batch of **Biden Documents** that the federal government attempted to keep hidden until we forced their release with our aggressive litigation and investigatory tactics. [**_Support us now to help expose Biden corruption_ >>>**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62479619:6Fb8V9GNO:m:1:3331421248:489951B05375DCE41F24010A5421B0DB:r) This **second trove of shocking documents** confirms there was effectively no daylight between Joe Biden and Hunter Biden regarding the Biden family's business dealings in Ukraine. But there is more. **These never-before-seen records and emails contain more explosive revelations** , _including:_ **#1 -** Then Vice President Biden personally signed off on the statement given in response to reporters about whether Hunter's appointment to **Burisma** undermines the Vice President's credibility in pushing anti-corruption measures in the Ukraine; **#2 -** The Office of the Vice President connected reporters writing about Hunter's appointment to**Burisma** to Amos Hochstein, a trusted Biden advisor who was then the "point person" for International Energy Affairs at the U.S. Department of State, to provide a "unique and valuable perspective" on the relationships in Ukraine; **#3 -** The Office of the Vice President coordinated closely with Hunter Biden's notorious firm **Rosemont Seneca Advisors** (the firm has been prominently involved in Hunter Biden's various international business dealings, which Joe Biden has claimed to know nothing about) in response to press inquiries; **#4 -** U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, Marie L. Yovanovitch, reported _- to the Department of State's Victoria Nuland, Anna Makanju in Vice President Biden's Office, and the National Security Counsel's Eric Ciaramella-_**that "this time there are accusations that Burisma allegedly had a subsidiary dump natural gas as a way to pay bribes" and "mentions that Hunter Biden and former Polish President Kwasniewski are on the Burisma board."** Taken together with the Oversight Committee's revelations about the Biden Family finances, **it is now _indisputable_ that there is a need for a full criminal investigation** into the Bidens' foreign influence peddling and exotic foreign financial payoffs. We now have the evidence in plain sight. _This can be ignored no longer!_
| [**HELP EXPOSE BIDEN >>**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62479619:6Fb8V9GNO:m:1:3331421248:489951B05375DCE41F24010A5421B0DB:r)
---
This is yet another example of how our hard work at America First Legal is paying off and getting results for _YOU_ , the American People. [**Please make a generous and urgently needed donation now to help my team continue the fight and expose Biden 's corruption in a court of law. ****_Please help us continue the investigation right now >> _****_ _**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62479619:6Fb8V9GNO:m:1:3331421248:489951B05375DCE41F24010A5421B0DB:r) _With deep gratitude for your continuously brave support,_ Stephen Miller President, America First Legal
|
---
Note: This hard evidence which shows the intertwinement of Hunter Biden's foreign dealings with then Vice President Joe Biden, _including proof that people inside Biden's Vice Presidential Office knew about and were worried about Biden's Burisma_ _dealings_ would not have been discovered without our lawsuits.**

| | | [**READ THE FULL STORY**](https://www.votedrbob.com/so/cfOSVAiEA/c?w=V4q-kXiDdkyobrZXNupBR_-qO6_0EBmaJkou1C747F8.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2FuZHJld2ZvcmJvYiIsInIiOiIyMGNmYzNlOC1iMDc3LTRhMDctODBhMS1iMDg0NzI1ZDI5NDkiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIxODY2MjZkNS1jYTVlLTQ2MzMtODM0YS1lODgzZDBiZGRkNzUifQ)
---
| | One of the greatest lessons I've learned from my father is compassion for the vulnerable. He and I grew up differently. His family struggled with bouts of poverty and sometimes relied on government assistance. But, as he would say, that perspective makes him blessed. Dr. Bob blessed me with some profound life lessons. One, I'll never forget... In 9th grade, a friend and I came home from school frustrated that our classmate did better than us on a test. They utilized a reading aide, and the teacher granted the student more time. "They don't need it. It's unfair!" My friend and I snickered jealously and poked fun at the other boy. In our unedified minds, they cheated, and we were victims. We were catty teens, 14 or 15, and our brains were still developing. Usually very quiet and casual at home, my dad then perked up. It was one of the only times he raised his voice at me. Really. "Both of you, right now, stop!" he said. "No. You don't know your classmate's situation or disability. They deserve the accomplishment, and it takes a lot of courage to ask for help when you need it. No one is less than you because of they overcame a disadvantage." My father also studied with monks for a summer in Germany and was taught only to talk when you have something important to say. To him, this was clearly important. It was one of my first lessons in the fairness of equity, and I never made that mistake again.
---
| | | [**READ THE FULL STORY**](https://www.votedrbob.com/so/cfOSVAiEA/c?w=le-XT6-hheNvH7fR4MWlmJpFkHWiZB5UL8nwLScyHWw.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2FuZHJld2ZvcmJvYiIsInIiOiI5NDMzYzBjMy01YmExLTQwYTgtOTUyNy1hYWI2YjhlYWFhOWIiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIxODY2MjZkNS1jYTVlLTQ2MzMtODM0YS1lODgzZDBiZGRkNzUifQ)

My dad's presumed Republican opponent is ultra-MAGA Republican. Against dark money and Super PACs, we're creating a grassroots movement in the Midwest that will ignite and inspire quality Democratic candidates up and down the ballot.

clean_body: "Lessons from my Father" | We think this piece says a lot about Doc's character and why he's running. Read the full story at actbl | | Can't see this message? | | | | | Happy Sunday, In case you missed son's post, here are a few paragraphs. | | "Lessons from my Father" | | | | We think this piece says a lot about Doc's character and why he's running. Read the full story at . — Team Lorinser | | | | | One of the greatest lessons I've learned from my father is compassion for the vulnerable. He and I grew up differently. His family struggled with bouts of poverty and sometimes relied on government assistance. But, as he would say, that perspective makes him blessed. Dr. Bob blessed me with some profound life lessons. One, I'll never forget... In 9th grade, a friend and I came home from school frustrated that our classmate did better than us on a test. They utilized a reading aide, and the teacher granted the student more time. "They don't need it. It's unfair!" My friend and I snickered jealously and poked fun at the other boy. In our unedified minds, they cheated, and we were victims. We were catty teens, 14 or 15, and our brains were still developing. Usually very quiet and casual at home, my dad then perked up. It was one of the only times he raised his voice at me. Really. "Both of you, right now, stop!" he said. "No. You don't know your classmate's situation or disability. They deserve the accomplishment, and it takes a lot of courage to ask for help when you need it. No one is less than you because of they overcame a disadvantage." My father also studied with monks for a summer in Germany and was taught only to talk when you have something important to say. To him, this was clearly important. It was one of my first lessons in the fairness of equity, and I never made that mistake again. | | | | | to get a person of integrity back into our Congressional seat? My dad's presumed Republican opponent is ultra-MAGA Republican. Against dark money and Super PACs, we're creating a grassroots movement in the Midwest that will ignite and inspire quality Democratic candidates up and down the ballot. | | If you've recently contributed, thank you. Please disregard our ask, accept our gratitude and consider this correspondence a campaign update. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Or send a check to: "Friends of Dr. Bob"PO Box 936Marquette, MI 49855 Learn more about my dad and his campaign at . ---
_He’s right!_ For years, Republican politicians have used anti-democratic tactics to win unearned power...and they have no plans of changing their strategy.

**We’ll be blunt, team: The future of free and fair elections in our country depends on how we respond to this ongoing threat.** A.G. Holder explained why:
**“Our democracy is still at risk.** We should not think that because we had a fairly good result in the last election that everything is fine.

There are still people who are determined **through gerrymandering, through voter suppression, through attacks on our election infrastructure** in our nation to try to come up with ways to hold on to power to which they are not entitled...

**That’s why we’ve been fighting to make sure the process is fair.”**
---
_He’s right about that, too._ As long as Republicans are committed to using map manipulation and attacks on voting rights to win elections, those of us who believe in democracy must remain _just as committed_ to fighting for a level playing field.
[**_The NDRC is 100% committed to this fight, team. Will you heed A.G. Holder’s words of warning and help us defeat Republican attacks on democracy with a donation of any amount today?_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/60781091/398160647/1887409474?amount=30&refcode=EM_RTI_20230326_FR_3Q_redlight922_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJiZDYwNGNiLTEwY2MtZWQxMS1hOGUwLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WwXQmygI2XiGtj5TIcUGXAwq4F_IJaw9U5X7s9bGWmU=)

subject: [Staff Feature] Have you met Rachel?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Arizona Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| | | We want you to know who we are... | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xTerfBqG0VHddyZFF79qWDL3jfMMv7xurqr8stBPvg3oXFFXaY9EWn8-YEM_arv8hlNJ9tPGQdTy9-yxHBJD9WHM0xTckyWBfmwF3aa6__4Qm71U0jFmiWA9ma5Ep3CiL1Yk4NDH9LSiu3tLkcQS0qGwt867C3B_m_-jbwXzYzcmgWnUfOzecMNSjJ8PYRcJlVmNYQM-J6KrpdykccGCEdHsVBeQoBZBXVs9OKsEaia7CHDZNuS6aV8_K0eOp8jXVhaffzPOOOSdTaAl40zVoY4YXBSepPduVqIC7D13wucsg9K8uCjDk9mtoLtUEfjMNCGO3bsyNe9q7Wx9PXqnBcc3BbFycoetWIMDB1S2IyfddxWGjwSwmNkNuhQ2qQT6fWaHddcQR-9mlkqMrd6iVNi46K9XWeSL6Qdy7Av-D_dsOQw7pl87u5IapczHLrRmY/3ut/OjbJtelPT-mieMLOGf1BNg/h0/hoV9-2rvwaS-n9VACqXlV2c7bk6zidZbLqxKwirI110)

---
| Hi, I'm Rachel Hood, the Political Deputy Director for the Arizona Democratic Party, and I wanted to tell you a little about myself.

I hold a Bachelor of Arts in Business Administration from the University of Washington and received an Indigenous Governance Certificate at the University of Arizona. As a Yavapai-Apache Nation citizen as well as being half Yavapai and half Navajo, I wanted to use my education within the Yavapai-Apache Reservation to better help Indigenous initiatives within my community.

Before being elected to Tribal Council, I proudly worked as the Executive Director of Distant Drums RV Resort and successfully held a seat on the Business Board of Directors for the Yavapai-Apache Nation overseeing three businesses in the Verde Valley. Using my business training, I modified the resort's Business Model and transformed it into one of the industry's most successful RV Resorts.

As a result of my educational and working experience, I firmly believe that tribes need to exercise self-governance and self-determination to strengthen Indigenous sovereign powers. That is what has led me to where I am today.

I now work at the Arizona Democratic Party as the Political Deputy Director, putting my experience to great use in helping to reach out to the state's Native communities. With 22 tribal communities across the state, I work hard to help the party empower tribes by strengthening their sovereignty and self-determination.

**[If you want to help other staffers and me at the Arizona Democratic Party, can you chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can? clean_body: | | | We want you to know who we are... | | | Hi, I'm Rachel Hood, the Political Deputy Director for the Arizona Democratic Party, and I wanted to tell you a little about myself. I hold a Bachelor of Arts in Business Administration from the University of Washington and received an Indigenous Governance Certificate at the University of Arizona. As a Yavapai-Apache Nation citizen as well as being half Yavapai and half Navajo, I wanted to use my education within the Yavapai-Apache Reservation to better help Indigenous initiatives within my community. Before being elected to Tribal Council, I proudly worked as the Executive Director of Distant Drums RV Resort and successfully held a seat on the Business Board of Directors for the Yavapai-Apache Nation overseeing three businesses in the Verde Valley. Using my business training, I modified the resort's Business Model and transformed it into one of the industry's most successful RV Resorts. As a result of my educational and working experience, I firmly believe that tribes need to exercise self-governance and self-determination to strengthen Indigenous sovereign powers. That is what has led me to where I am today. I now work at the Arizona Democratic Party as the Political Deputy Director, putting my experience to great use in helping to reach out to the state's Native communities. With 22 tribal communities across the state, I work hard to help the party empower tribes by strengthening their sovereignty and self-determination. Thanks, Rachel Hood Paid for by the Arizona Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Rick McGuire Treasurer. Arizona Democratic Party 2910 North Central Ave Phoenix AZ 85012 United States | | Sent via . ---
Fellow patriot,

I’m reaching out to say thank you for your recent support for The League for Law Enforcement!

“The League” is a registered nonprofit political organization and Hybrid PAC organized under Section 527 with the IRS. **We believe that supporting trained, responsible law enforcement officers, in tandem with an armed citizenry, is essential to maintaining a free and civilized society.**

Unfortunately, the woke Left, Black Lives Matter, Antifa, and radical politicians are waging a War on Cops that aims to demonize, defund, and disarm law enforcement.

The result is rising crime and increased targeting of officers.

**We must restore law & order in America, and it starts by supporting law enforcement.**

We’re proud to partner with high profile law enforcement advocates like Sheriff David Clarke, Sheriff Joe Arpaio and others in raising awareness, influencing policy decisions, impacting election outcomes, and standing in the fray to defend law enforcement.

Support from patriots like you is critical to our mission and this fight, and we’re grateful to have yours!

For those who wear the badge,

**Jim Fotis** , President

* * *
“The League”
Lynbrook, NY PD 14-yr Veteran (retired)

[ **DONATE » **](http://links.theleaguealerts.com/u/click?_t=4d3358f89ac84254ae0da9863c6c378a&_m=05353377205a4674a7dffb47ba0f918b&_e=LeHrGr_7yU2S5oREe0usCy4TCrSLwiUkKYLgTKdO52zRJDQ9sKxA6gkV8F6q3CURqXTctRGALXyfHaPvpego5X6oJqQIh_5_9s4_bta2EJ1YzP9pTjG53pdM95gc95Qiq6blD3Sboiw43tMEncH4rtbhhFeQGTe6CCd3jC6MzpFAcWNeM_nAIWIBHDVk1MtTo63WVic0vYFFAOHmAUT_Bbu3-HQzuL8qkGmY1du1eD_3foPsxxFrP_LnMF1FF59sFXA_oVtJ79ibTo27rxWWsKmEHqzcMe0OY7BpekC7LcC7FBsYU7ZMjFXaYtAmIEBR89XYclIwuoWPQPiahORSaw%3D%3D)

| |
# ABOUT "THE LEAGUE"
---
The League was formed to bring back the law - both in the inalienable rights of defense and in enforcing the laws at all levels of government, protecting the good and removing the bad from our great society. The League represents sportsmen, hunters, law enforcement, and others who in the past have been the mainstay of our society. The League stands for the defense of our Constitutional Rights, our inalienable rights protected by the First Amendment, Second Amendment and the eight other amendments in our Bill of Rights.
# ABOUT JIM FOTIS

A retired 14-year veteran of the Lynbrook Police Department in New York, Jim Fotis was injured in the line of duty and is the highest decorated officer in the Department's history. Now, Jim is dedicating his life to advocating for law enforcement and American values.
## [**JOIN US TODAY »**](http://links.theleaguealerts.com/u/click?_t=4d3358f89ac84254ae0da9863c6c378a&_m=05353377205a4674a7dffb47ba0f918b&_e=LeHrGr_7yU2S5oREe0usCy4TCrSLwiUkKYLgTKdO52zRJDQ9sKxA6gkV8F6q3CURqXTctRGALXyfHaPvpego5X6oJqQIh_5_9s4_bta2EJ1YzP9pTjG53pdM95gc95Qiq6blD3Sboiw43tMEncH4rtbhhFeQGTe6CCd3jC6MzpHxjHhBdaU2TtusWFWzMNUXeUjcFHvj01ULUr_OZUblB51j4CzyCGvUO7rqdE886-C-CT4-nf_c8IH-woC7WmgKgwj4ISF1gFJ515b2d5x3xjZlyA7ai9DdlCIlz-SEiboQm18dKOLbLJLac4WQCd6RB6kh0Zjev90Taed_0J2ZCA%3D%3D)

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Trump appointed Judge, Matthew J. Kacsmaryk, will almost definitively overturn the FDA approved, extremely safe/popular abortion pill, Mifepristone. And it's so much worse than we thought.
body:
Abortion pill access gone, just like that!
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcf46613d-c2a9-48e0-acbc-b6430d8c9950/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, **_We thought we'd heard the worst about Trump appointed Judge, Matthew J. Kacsmaryk, who'll almost definitively overturn the FDA approved, extremely safe/popular abortion pill, Mifepristone. But it's so much worse than we thought._** **It turns out Kacsmaryk doesn't even care to hide he's a right-wing advocate, even as a "judge." Erin Morrow Hawley'****_s one of the lawyers leading the charge vs. the FDA to have the abortion pill banned._ If the name sounds familiar, it's because _her husband is repulsive, seditionist Senator Josh Hawley._** **_Wanna guess to whose campaign this "judge" just donated? Josh Hawley, of course(!), the husband of a lawyer appearing before him in court._** **_We can't allow this extremist to hijack women's rights. We maybe can't stop his decision, but we sure as hell can have it overturned on appeal.[_So,_](https://default.salsalabs.org/T235c4111-0566-4961-b82f-77e6e075ae4b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)_[_ w_](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf654d791-2f1e-4f2d-b286-8cadb1092469/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**[** _ill you join us in donating $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to the Center for Reproductive Rights' Abortion Defense Network to fight this attack on our basic rights?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5d70819f-0ab1-4e7f-88e3-0b72dbc9da4f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T182eac59-00dd-46dd-a7fb-79d7f0520d43/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| _Kacsmaryk even has a bizarre obsession with the sexuality of others, referring to gay Americans as "disordered" and saying the "Sexual Revolution" was about how "...marriage, sexuality, gender identity, and even the unborn child must yield to the erotic desires of liberated adults." Get the man in therapy, and away from women's bodies._ **_We can't allow this extremist to hijack women's rights, but we sure as hell can work to have it overturned on appeal.[_So,_](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2ac121cd-8528-4870-83ac-92fa99dda274/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)_[_ w_](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta1f9a6bf-23f4-4144-a34f-e850b8df1b9f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**[** _ill you join us in donating $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to the Center for Reproductive Rights' Abortion Defense Network to fight this attack on our basic rights?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tae86ff5e-328c-4794-ac45-894a4f2de25f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Thank you for never giving in to extremists who think they get to substitute their warped judgment for that of well-adjusted human beings._ Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3612de2b-a777-4c2f-8fa5-f5e44faab931/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Mohona Chowdhury
subject: building our war chest
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| As Sharice’s campaign manager, I’m focused on building up our campaign and making sure we have the resources to fight back in what we know will be another competitive race next year.

Sharice is officially a **top target for Republicans** who want to expand their slim House majority. In 2022, they poured millions of dollars into misleading attack ads against Sharice, and we know to expect that to continue ahead of 2024’s election.

This Friday is our first FEC filing deadline since I became campaign manager. It also marks our first big deadline since Sharice was named a top target. Reporting weak numbers isn’t an option as prospective opponents decide if they want to challenge us.

**[This is the biggest moment of the election so far—and as a campaign veteran, I can tell you it’s only going to get harder from here. Are we going to prove that we’re up to the challenges ahead? Click here to chip in $20 or any amount to help us hit our FEC goal of raising another $20,000 before midnight Friday.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60782199/398166342/-1334289778?amount=20&contactdata=9oPORNqV0giOwt+0UoyiCqXliQlrOwFr0zWUPrxnxaQj7nfJACQslN9QX4Wfa4ZnYF2U5KR45hw1+LT3++iFLfLgmjwy/BmyBebP3WbkaoyaBNQ9pChgQj/7jrUvp4NHId6S+aw8jQt6q13Yz2UAFg==&source=EM_NBI_SD_20230326_FR_1.3_0_1MO_cm_NA_NA_eoq&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjgwYzA2Yi0xOWNjLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vrY6GuDtXz5S7mwHtjvjMsfnvPsNfTw8bzZZVHxI2ak=)** | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately.

clean_body: | As Sharice’s campaign manager, I’m focused on building up our campaign and making sure we have the resources to fight back in what we know will be another competitive race next year. Sharice is officially a top target for Republicans who want to expand their slim House majority. In 2022, they poured millions of dollars into misleading attack ads against Sharice, and we know to expect that to continue ahead of 2024’s election. This Friday is our first FEC filing deadline since I became campaign manager. It also marks our first big deadline since Sharice was named a top target. Reporting weak numbers isn’t an option as prospective opponents decide if they want to challenge us. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | = | = | = | = | = | = If we can hit this crucial FEC goal by Friday, we’ll show Republicans and their dark money donors that Sharice has the grassroots strength to win another competitive race and keep KS-03 blue in 2024. = Thanks, Mohona Campaign Manager, Team Sharice * | = | Click here to from Sharice Davids' email list. | | Hi folks, it’s Susie Lee. **$26.**

I'm proud to share that $26 is the average grassroots donation to our campaign, friend.
I know that might not seem like much when you think about the tens of millions that GOP super PACs are going to spend to try to defeat me and flip the Senate. But that's the power of this grassroots movement – it's made up of folks like you who believe in this fight and chip in what they can, when they can.
This movement is how we're going to defy the pundits who are calling me one of the most vulnerable senators in the country, win in Ohio, and defend our Senate majority._ _

But here's the problem: **_The Cook Political Report_ ranks this race a toss-up – and Washington Republicans are already launching attack ads against me. We need all hands on deck to fight back – but right now, we're coming up short.**

**If everyone reading this email chipped in $26 – or even just a few dollars – we could reach our $15,000 fundraising goal before March 26th ends. But not everyone will, so I'm personally asking if I can count on you, friend.**
**[Please, will you chip in $26 – or as much as you can right now – to help me fight back against GOP attacks, defend this seat, and keep the Senate blue?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60782538/398168089/691330013?amounts=5,26,50,100,250&amount=26&refcode=jtk80-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYWJiNmYxMC0xZWNjLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=s3FY1JQvi_IiH5uqvYrwO-AzkVZY9aDKSG7tIQfphi4=)**

clean_body: Join the fight today | Friends, Americans continue to pay for President Biden's reckless spending. I am DETERMINED to put a STOP to this madness before it is too late! | The cost of food and gas are the highest they’ve been in FOUR decades. I will NEVER stop working hard to cut spending, balance the budget, stop reckless spending, and make America Energy Independent. I know it seems wrong to email you asking for money when we are trying to stop inflation, but the only way to get our point across is by stopping the Democrats in their tracks. Far-Left Democrats get voted into office and forget about their constituents. It is clear NOW MORE THAN EVER that Joe Biden and his administration have put America and our well-being LAST on their agenda. The only way to stop reckless spending and the radical Left’s failed agenda is by standing together and fighting back. God Bless, | ---|--- Paid for by Banks for Senate Copyright (C) 2023 Banks for Senate, All rights reserved.

name: Mariah Morgan
subject: “These House Democrats should be shaking in their boots.”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email]Friends,
I have some serious news: both mine and Don Davis’ seats are vulnerable in 2024.
We’re coming together to add fuel to both of our campaigns and prepare for whatever attacks national Republicans are planning to send our way. Can you rush in a $30 split donation to support both of our campaigns? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email&amount=30]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
RUSH IN $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email&express_lane=true&amount=10] RUSH IN $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email&express_lane=true&amount=25]
RUSH IN $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email&express_lane=true&amount=50] RUSH IN $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email&express_lane=true&amount=100]
RUSH IN $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email&express_lane=true&amount=250] RUSH IN ANY AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email]
Our goal in 2024 is to take back the House. To make it happen, we have to hold on to both of our seats in North Carolina. Both Don and I are focused on fighting for North Carolina families in DC. From affordable health care to revitalizing our economy, we both are working day in and day out to deliver results.
The GOP has made it clear that they are not only going to try and hold on to the House, but expand their majority in 2024. They have a plan to flip back all of the seats that won by less than 5% of the vote, which includes mine and Don’s seat.
This is serious, and the sooner we build up both of our campaign funds, the better chance we have to combat their efforts. So do your part to keep both of our seats BLUE by making a split donation today! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn-split?refcode=230302-email]
Thank you so much, team.
Wiley Nickel
Wiley Nickel is the Congressman for NC-13. His 2022 opponent, Bo Hines, has announced he'll run against Wiley in 2024. Support #TeamWiley with a monthly donation today!

name: Katie Porter
subject: Rigged
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
I’m running to bring oversight and accountability to the U.S. Senate.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)
Derek,
I’m running to bring oversight and accountability to the U.S. Senate. Here’s what that means:
* Holding Members of Congress accountable to the people they represent by **banning Members from trading stocks.**
* Overseeing how taxpayer dollars are spent so we **aren’t subsidizing Big Oil companies.**
* **Holding those with power to account:** whether it’s a gun manufacturer, a bank CEO, or a government official.

But I need your help to take our fight (and my whiteboard!) to the Senate. [**Can you chip in before our March 31 end of quarter deadline?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230326-1?refcode=EMAF26_1_EOQ_20230326_V1_F&refcode2=20062_3422442_7DCcQ9&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)
**[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230326-1?refcode=EMAF26_1_EOQ_20230326_V1_F&refcode2=20062_3422442_7DCcQ9&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)**
Before being elected to Congress in 2018 I was a consumer protection attorney. **I’ve seen how our country is rigged _against_ working people, and how big corporations can cheat while working people play by the rules and struggle to get ahead.** That’s why I’m so passionate about good oversight. So passionate in fact, that I put it on my license plate!
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230326-1?refcode=EMAF26_1_EOQ_20230326_V1_F&refcode2=20062_3422442_7DCcQ9&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)
Oversight is often about closing the gap between what the law says on paper and what actually happens in people's lives. That wide gap is leading to an erosion of trust between Americans and our democracy.
Consider making a donation today to help power our movement. **Our campaign’s very first end of quarter deadline is next Friday, so now is one of the most useful times for you to make a contribution.**
**[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230326-1?refcode=EMAF26_1_EOQ_20230326_V1_F&refcode2=20062_3422442_7DCcQ9&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)**
Thanks so much, Derek.
Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAF26_1_EOQ_20230326_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=20062_3422442_7DCcQ9&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4s-emails/?t=3&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-update-info-footer)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=20062%2E3422442%2E7DCcQ9)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Senate
PO Box 5176
Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate

Legislative Update for Week Ending March 25, 2003
This update will cover the last two weeks. Due to the SCC election in Gering last weekend we were unable to produce last week’s edition. Let’s get started. Pro-LIfe Last week an amendment was brought forward to change the original language from 6 weeks (approximately when a heartbeat begins) to 12 weeks. This proposal created an emotional episode at the Capitol as well as within the party. Collaborative discussion between Senators addressed concerns to get this bill back on track. Meanwhile, at the SCC convention, a resolution that was already in the works to affirm the heartbeat bill was amended and passed by an overwhelming support of the body. The amendment offered and affirmed that the SCC governing body did not support an extension of the time frame of the bill beyond the terms written in the original bill. Kids Take Center Stage This week saw the Capitol overrun with people as the Unicameral took action on the Let Them Grow Act (LB 574). In addition, committee hearings were held on banning access to porn in schools by installing filters and prohibiting involvement of children with Drag shows. Good news...we advanced LB 574 to Select file! Changing one’s sex is not “Gender Affirming” or “Healthcare” (as Wyoming just redefined). Gender affirming is preserving one’s sex before one is an adult and healthcare is not taking actions to put aspects of your health at risk. Don’t buy into the progressive media’s redefinition of words for the benefit of their handlers. The GOP is committed to protecting our children and the legal precedents that children will not be considered the same as adults for very solid and logical reasons. As usual, the Democrats define everything as hate. The 2023 Republican will not be deterred by this claim made at every hour of the day by the Filibuster Party. As a sarcastic contrast we would expect the opponents of these bills to bring ridiculous legislation forward to make...it legal for children to drink...be prosecuted the same as adults...lowering the age to be drafted to war...giving them the option to not go to school...and many more decisions that adults make in our society. The arguments are absurd, inaccurate, and most importantly inappropriate. The visuals and the discussions being had on the floor were downright embarrassing. One teacher wisely removed her class from listening to the offensive discussions in the pews in the balcony. There is no hate. There are just wise decisions being made for children before they are of age to make complete rational decisions for themselves. Strong efforts were made by the party and elected officials to preserve LB 574 from last week to advancing it on Thursday. There is plenty of work yet to be done for complete passage. Finally, the GOP passed a Parental Bill of Rights in DC. Unfortunately, the Parental Bill of Rights is struggling in Nebraska. The DC bill seeks to restore parental power over their kids and not the school. Budget review, rights to speak, and curriculum review were all in the bill. We need parental rights preserved at a national and state level that are defined in detail to prevent future redefining of words.
Winner Take All Senator Loren Lippincott introduced a bill to make Nebraska match most other states in regards to the Electoral College. The winner of the state would take all electoral college votes and would no longer be split. The committee has yet to act on that bill. More Educational Outrage in Omaha This letter shed light on the actions the Papillion board was taking against parents by teaching their methods to suppress parental concerns at a National Conference in Orlando. This week the controversy is at Omaha Westside. Undercover reporters exposed the plans to continue SEL and CRT lessons by using new words such as “Belonging” to teach the same concepts. We need CRT/Racism out of our schools. [https://youtu.be/R62bFu_jSXg](https://www.ne.gop/so/56OSVgn49/c?w=sbwv2_Ig7aM6I2rWSpSHkesU-dnogIzhcyDG1HUnG54.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9SNjJiRnVfalNYZyIsInIiOiJhNmViODU0Ny04NzZiLTQ1ZWItYzU1OC03M2ViMDg2ZGM5ZWEiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI0MThjMTc2MS1lZGQzLTRjMWEtYjA2NC1jMGJhYzlkMDI2NGYifQ) Statements were also made that they had large banking families and the Chamber’s influence down at the Unicameral to get their way. We have not validated those statements, but are concerned about other groups doing the bidding of these boards as stated by this administrator. Republicans should continue as a unified party to emphasize school boards at all levels. Parents, not school boards, should dictate the education of their kids. The NEGOP will continue to listen to the parents in schooling of their children and will not put the interests of the powerful ahead of what is right. Finally, we remain concerned about education on the whole. The legislative research office reported an English proficiency rate of 28% and a math proficiency rate of 20% for Omaha Public Schools. The ACT scores of under 16 and an English ACT score of less than 14 was alarming for a district spending over $700 million and has the resources for a student to teacher ratio of 15. It is apparent education needs serious work in our largest city, yet we find these similar statistics occurring in rural communities as well. Republicans need to continue to seek change for the benefit of Nebraska's children. New ideas need to be addressed and new leaders need to be found to change the trajectory of outcomes that affirms equality not equity, a focus on improved performance, and a respect for values being taught at home...as opposed to radical ideology being spread by schools. The NEGOP
The NEGOP held their Constitutionally required, biannual leadership elections in Gering last weekend. The weather was perfect and we thank the hosts and volunteers who made the warm welcome to CD3. Chairman Eric Underwood was unanimously reelected by the SCC. Each of the Congressional districts held their leadership elections. There were changes in various leadership roles for all three districts. We are grateful for the contributions of our past and current leaders to make this party stronger. Thank you! The party elected a new Treasurer in Dave Plond (Grand Island) who urged the need for financial support. Donate and support these efforts! Thank you to outgoing Treasurer Mike Kennedy of Omaha for all your leadership and guidance over the past eight months. Resolutions were brought forward and voted on. The support for EPIC tax was reaffirmed, a call to free those still in custody from January 6th was affirmed, the Heartbeat bill was officially supported and an amendment was brought affirming that the party did not support extending the number of weeks in the Heartbeat bill. Gracious sponsorship came from Congressman Adrian Smith for the Booster Social on Friday night. Attorney General Mike Hilgers sponsored breakfast and spoke of the many legal battles he and his office are fighting at the National level for Nebraskans. Senator Brian Hardin of LD 48 welcomed everyone to the Panhandle and both Senator Hardin and Senator Steve Erdman gave speeches on Unicameral updates. Lunchtime speakers were Public Service Commissioner Kevin Stocker, State Board of Education member Elizabeth Tegtmeier, and UNL Board of Regents Kathy Wilmot who all provided updates on their respective offices. Rod Edwards spoke for Charles W. Herbster of Conklin who sponsored lunch and yummy desserts. Political Director Todd Watson and Legislative Director Will Feely, gave updates on the Legislative Plan as passed by the body and a preview of issues that will have an effect on the 2024 election cycle. Rules of the Unicameral; Productivity As we discussed in previous editions and was addressed in the SCC-adopted Legislative Plan, the Rules of the Unicameral can dictate the efficiency and productivity of the session. Currently, ZERO bills have made it through Select File to be presented to Governor Pillen. It is indicated that there will be an amendment to the Unicameral rules debated on Tuesday in the hope to make this session more productive. There are different opinions amongst the body of what needs to be changed in the rules. We look forward to seeing where the consensus lands on Tuesday when they reconvene. These changes could have a dramatic effect on the trajectory of this session. Without doing something, the productivity of this session is severely limited based on the liberal leadership of the Filibuster Party. Please pray for the updated rules to be passed so this can be a productive session for Nebraska. Taxes Tax packages continue to move forward on property tax relief. LB 243 moved out of Committee. Senator Briese’s bill will increase property tax relief from $315 to $388 billion in 2024 and eventually $560 million by 2029.
LB 589 advanced and seeks to cap School spending at 3% a year (unless an override by 75% of the school board occurs). The state also advanced removing community college taxes to the state and off the property tax rolls. Finally, Senator Riepe introduced his Constitutional Amendment to repeal the Inheritance Tax. The Republican Party Platform supports this measure and lets Nebraskans decide if we should continue this tax only 5 states deploy. Election Security The media, Senators, and everyone opposed to Voter ID are doing their best to water down election security in this cycle. Regardless, we continue to hear key bills on securing the election. If there is one issue of consensus that separates the people and the legislature...it is Election Accountability and Voter Verification. The NEGOP will continue to represent the Platform, the Legislative Plan, and express the will of the people to seek strong security measures in our elections. The government committee continues to hear bills on machines being made in America, secure ballots with watermarks, and third party audits of the work of the Secretary of State with many noting that our elections lack third party verification outside of that office. However, as the progress of this session slows, the focus continues to shift to the Voter ID bill in a limited action environment. Republicans and the 65% who voted last year for a Photo Voter ID law should be concerned about the final version of that bill. The Legislative Committee volunteers have invested thousands of hours into those bills that have suddenly gone dark. The NEGOP stands behind the collaborative work in Senator Erdman’s ID bill. This bill reflects the true will of the people who have given a voice at our meetings and it is designed in a manner to create a secure, fair, and a Constitutionally honored outcome (validate the ID upon receipt of the ballot). The Constitutional Amendment MUST BE OBSERVED and that means a verification of the ID for every ballot WHEN TURNED IN. We anticipate a new ID bill from Senator Slama to be released soon with new language. Stay tuned.... Water Water resources has been in many conversations and pieces of legislation. Bills from around the state are seeking to address nitrates, expand capacity, build lines from the Missouri into Lincoln, and address the Perkins canal. Although most of the bills don’t really relate from one to another, it is clear that Nebraskans have different concerns on water for different reasons in different areas of the state. Our Political Plan for 2023-24 will put a greater focus on NRD boards. I’m seeking a volunteer political advisor that will be solely focused on these important races around the state. It is imperative our Republican county chairs are briefed on the important people running these races. Furthermore, I’m calling on the citizens to start paying more attention to these critical races. Water is a big concern for different reasons to all Nebraskans. Water is critical to life. We will be seeking more information and focus to ensure our natural resources remain in a good place for all of Nebraska.
2nd Amendment We are anticipating the vote for Constitutional Carry to be on Tuesday this week. The NRA will be providing a lunch at the NEGOP headquarters at 1610 N St. in Lincoln before the floor vote. Lunch will start at 11:30. Come join other freedom loving Americans before the vote for a bite to eat! A Caution on Spending In the coming weeks the NEGOP will be taking a look at all the new spending proposals coming from both parties. There IS merit to some new spending to solve critical problems. However, the fiscal footnotes continue to show a little here, a little there, a lot here, a lot there, and boom you have a new level of government spending. Our platform for Republicans is clear we are for lower taxes and fiscal responsibility. Lower taxes can only occur with lower spending. The Governor has been clear that he wants to scrub every dollar for efficiency. We agree with that assessment. The state is blessed with a large surplus. However, we believe that surplus belongs to the people. We are encouraging our Senators to look at the “Whole” of new spending and see where cuts can be made and ask if we really need this expenditure or if that increase to an existing program is wise. Lower spending enables lower taxes. Psychologically, we know more money in the wallet creates loose spending. We need to overcome that psychology and take a hard look at the new proposals. "Do we really need this?", should be the primary question. We need to remain diligent on spending so that we can lower tax rates and be competitive in the region for the long haul. Banking Problems The economic world was stunned when two of the largest banks collapsed. The response by Biden and the Federal Reserve has damaged the future trajectory of the banks that support Nebraska. The actions of the Federal Reserve backstopped all the deposits of customers over 250k of those large banks in large urban hubs. 250k is the amount insured by the FDIC. They elected to supersede those limits. Meanwhile, Senator Lankford of Oklahoma grilled Yellen that it was unfair that a smaller bank was not bailed out and was allowed to fail by the Federal Reserve. This action continues to show the favoritism and Inequality Under the Law practiced by our government. The decision communicated to the market that your deposits are safe at the large institutions (Class Shareholders of the Fed typically) and not safe at the smaller banks. This message being communicated is having depositors question where they will bank their deposits “To Be Safe”. A reduction of deposits to smaller banks will hurt our local institutions and economy. It is these smaller banks that are the bedrock of Nebraska communities from loans to the sponsorships of Little League Teams. It is imperative that our federal delegation restore Equality Before the Law for the sake of our local banks that make our local communities run. Big banks winning and small banks losing just show how out of touch the Fed and this President is with rural America and bedrock concepts of Equality Before the Law. Meanwhile, the Central Bank continues to push through laws in every state of the land. Controversy developed when Governor Noem put a stop to the bill in South Dakota that yielded the power of the people and currencies of their choice (not subject to the printing press) to Central Bank control. You can watch that video here. LB 214 carried by Senator Slama is the Nebraska version of the bill Governor Noem vetoed. Senator Slama promised to look into every line of that bill after the controversy took hold in Nebraska. The NEGOP supports equality before the law and a return of understanding of the Constitution as stated in Article I, Section 8, Clause 5. Monetary policy that has drifted from Constitutional understanding is having dramatic consequences. We encourage our Federal Delegation to fight for the interests of equality in all banking and the freedom of the people to choose currencies not manipulated by the printing press since the Congress has relegated its power to the Federal Reserve. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WVfRzWlmiXg](https://www.ne.gop/so/56OSVgn49/c?w=OqNftG4Y3wroIwVdASHmr9y1lkY4UlYZjQpZVSa2eXA.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj1XVmZSeldsbWlYZyIsInIiOiJhNmViODU0Ny04NzZiLTQ1ZWItYzU1OC03M2ViMDg2ZGM5ZWEiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI0MThjMTc2MS1lZGQzLTRjMWEtYjA2NC1jMGJhYzlkMDI2NGYifQ) Special Acknowledgment The NEGOP wishes to recognize Senator Ray Aguilar and Senator Mike Jacobsen for their votes. Senator Aguilar showed true grit and determination as he was brought into the chamber in a wheelchair with severe discomfort due to knee surgery. Even in pain, he cast a critical vote this week. In a culture where we hear complaints of how it should be made easier to vote...we saw the duty and the value of having the right to vote, by Senator Aguilar, who endured to push the button. Absolutely amazing. Senator Jacobsen faced a stiff fine for not being at an important banking governance meeting so he could be here to vote. To subject himself to a personal fine to stand for the kids of Nebraska does not go unnoticed. The sacrifices for kids and principles is inspiring. Despite the chaos the media wants you to see, we found the moments that really inspire and show our true values. We don’t see hate. We see a love of kids, and people willing to pay a price for their long term interests at the Unicameral this week. Until Next Week... Todd WatsonNEGOP Political Director political@ne.gop

Meanwhile, at the SCC convention, a resolution that was already in the works to affirm the heartbeat bill was amended and passed by an overwhelming support of the body. The amendment offered and affirmed that the SCC governing body did not support an extension of the time frame of the bill beyond the terms written in the original bill. Kids Take Center Stage This week saw the Capitol overrun with people as the Unicameral took action on the Let Them Grow Act (LB 574). In addition, committee hearings were held on banning access to porn in schools by installing filters and prohibiting involvement of children with Drag shows. Good news...we advanced LB 574 to Select file! Changing one’s sex is not “Gender Affirming” or “Healthcare” (as Wyoming just redefined). Gender affirming is preserving one’s sex before one is an adult and healthcare is not taking actions to put aspects of your health at risk. Don’t buy into the progressive media’s redefinition of words for the benefit of their handlers. The GOP is committed to protecting our children and the legal precedents that children will not be considered the same as adults for very solid and logical reasons. As usual, the Democrats define everything as hate. The 2023 Republican will not be deterred by this claim made at every hour of the day by the Filibuster Party. As a sarcastic contrast we would expect the opponents of these bills to bring ridiculous legislation forward to make...it legal for children to drink...be prosecuted the same as adults...lowering the age to be drafted to war...giving them the option to not go to school...and many more decisions that adults make in our society. The arguments are absurd, inaccurate, and most importantly inappropriate. The visuals and the discussions being had on the floor were downright embarrassing. One teacher wisely removed her class from listening to the offensive discussions in the pews in the balcony. There is no hate. There are just wise decisions being made for children before they are of age to make complete rational decisions for themselves. Strong efforts were made by the party and elected officials to preserve LB 574 from last week to advancing it on Thursday. There is plenty of work yet to be done for complete passage. Finally, the GOP passed a Parental Bill of Rights in DC. Unfortunately, the Parental Bill of Rights is struggling in Nebraska. The DC bill seeks to restore parental power over their kids and not the school. Budget review, rights to speak, and curriculum review were all in the bill. We need parental rights preserved at a national and state level that are defined in detail to prevent future redefining of words. Winner Take All Senator Loren Lippincott introduced a bill to make Nebraska match most other states in regards to the Electoral College. The winner of the state would take all electoral college votes and would no longer be split. The committee has yet to act on that bill. More Educational Outrage in Omaha This letter shed light on the actions the Papillion board was taking against parents by teaching their methods to suppress parental concerns at a National Conference in Orlando. This week the controversy is at Omaha Westside. Undercover reporters exposed the plans to continue SEL and CRT lessons by using new words such as “Belonging” to teach the same concepts. We need CRT/Racism out of our schools. Statements were also made that they had large banking families and the Chamber’s influence down at the Unicameral to get their way. We have not validated those statements, but are concerned about other groups doing the bidding of these boards as stated by this administrator. Republicans should continue as a unified party to emphasize school boards at all levels. Parents, not school boards, should dictate the education of their kids. The NEGOP will continue to listen to the parents in schooling of their children and will not put the interests of the powerful ahead of what is right. Finally, we remain concerned about education on the whole. The legislative research office reported an English proficiency rate of 28% and a math proficiency rate of 20% for Omaha Public Schools. The ACT scores of under 16 and an English ACT score of less than 14 was alarming for a district spending over $700 million and has the resources for a student to teacher ratio of 15. It is apparent education needs serious work in our largest city, yet we find these similar statistics occurring in rural communities as well. Republicans need to continue to seek change for the benefit of Nebraska's children. New ideas need to be addressed and new leaders need to be found to change the trajectory of outcomes that affirms equality not equity, a focus on improved performance, and a respect for values being taught at home...as opposed to radical ideology being spread by schools. The NEGOP The NEGOP held their Constitutionally required, biannual leadership elections in Gering last weekend. The weather was perfect and we thank the hosts and volunteers who made the warm welcome to CD3. Chairman Eric Underwood was unanimously reelected by the SCC. Each of the Congressional districts held their leadership elections. There were changes in various leadership roles for all three districts. We are grateful for the contributions of our past and current leaders to make this party stronger. Thank you! The party elected a new Treasurer in Dave Plond (Grand Island) who urged the need for financial support. Donate and support these efforts! Thank you to outgoing Treasurer Mike Kennedy of Omaha for all your leadership and guidance over the past eight months. Resolutions were brought forward and voted on. The support for EPIC tax was reaffirmed, a call to free those still in custody from January 6th was affirmed, the Heartbeat bill was officially supported and an amendment was brought affirming that the party did not support extending the number of weeks in the Heartbeat bill. Gracious sponsorship came from Congressman Adrian Smith for the Booster Social on Friday night. Attorney General Mike Hilgers sponsored breakfast and spoke of the many legal battles he and his office are fighting at the National level for Nebraskans. Senator Brian Hardin of LD 48 welcomed everyone to the Panhandle and both Senator Hardin and Senator Steve Erdman gave speeches on Unicameral updates. Lunchtime speakers were Public Service Commissioner Kevin Stocker, State Board of Education member Elizabeth Tegtmeier, and UNL Board of Regents Kathy Wilmot who all provided updates on their respective offices. Rod Edwards spoke for Charles W. Herbster of Conklin who sponsored lunch and yummy desserts. Political Director Todd Watson and Legislative Director Will Feely, gave updates on the Legislative Plan as passed by the body and a preview of issues that will have an effect on the 2024 election cycle. Rules of the Unicameral; Productivity As we discussed in previous editions and was addressed in the SCC-adopted Legislative Plan, the Rules of the Unicameral can dictate the efficiency and productivity of the session. Currently, ZERO bills have made it through Select File to be presented to Governor Pillen. It is indicated that there will be an amendment to the Unicameral rules debated on Tuesday in the hope to make this session more productive. There are different opinions amongst the body of what needs to be changed in the rules. We look forward to seeing where the consensus lands on Tuesday when they reconvene. These changes could have a dramatic effect on the trajectory of this session. Without doing something, the productivity of this session is severely limited based on the liberal leadership of the Filibuster Party. Please pray for the updated rules to be passed so this can be a productive session for Nebraska. Taxes Tax packages continue to move forward on property tax relief. LB 243 moved out of Committee. Senator Briese’s bill will increase property tax relief from $315 to $388 billion in 2024 and eventually $560 million by 2029. LB 589 advanced and seeks to cap School spending at 3% a year (unless an override by 75% of the school board occurs). The state also advanced removing community college taxes to the state and off the property tax rolls. Finally, Senator Riepe introduced his Constitutional Amendment to repeal the Inheritance Tax. The Republican Party Platform supports this measure and lets Nebraskans decide if we should continue this tax only 5 states deploy. Election Security The media, Senators, and everyone opposed to Voter ID are doing their best to water down election security in this cycle. Regardless, we continue to hear key bills on securing the election. If there is one issue of consensus that separates the people and the legislature...it is Election Accountability and Voter Verification. The NEGOP will continue to represent the Platform, the Legislative Plan, and express the will of the people to seek strong security measures in our elections. The government committee continues to hear bills on machines being made in America, secure ballots with watermarks, and third party audits of the work of the Secretary of State with many noting that our elections lack third party verification outside of that office. However, as the progress of this session slows, the focus continues to shift to the Voter ID bill in a limited action environment. Republicans and the 65% who voted last year for a Photo Voter ID law should be concerned about the final version of that bill. The Legislative Committee volunteers have invested thousands of hours into those bills that have suddenly gone dark. The NEGOP stands behind the collaborative work in Senator Erdman’s ID bill. This bill reflects the true will of the people who have given a voice at our meetings and it is designed in a manner to create a secure, fair, and a Constitutionally honored outcome (validate the ID upon receipt of the ballot). The Constitutional Amendment MUST BE OBSERVED and that means a verification of the ID for every ballot WHEN TURNED IN. We anticipate a new ID bill from Senator Slama to be released soon with new language. Stay tuned.... Water Water resources has been in many conversations and pieces of legislation. Bills from around the state are seeking to address nitrates, expand capacity, build lines from the Missouri into Lincoln, and address the Perkins canal. Although most of the bills don’t really relate from one to another, it is clear that Nebraskans have different concerns on water for different reasons in different areas of the state. Our Political Plan for 2023-24 will put a greater focus on NRD boards. I’m seeking a volunteer political advisor that will be solely focused on these important races around the state. It is imperative our Republican county chairs are briefed on the important people running these races. Furthermore, I’m calling on the citizens to start paying more attention to these critical races. Water is a big concern for different reasons to all Nebraskans. Water is critical to life. We will be seeking more information and focus to ensure our natural resources remain in a good place for all of Nebraska. 2nd Amendment We are anticipating the vote for Constitutional Carry to be on Tuesday this week. The NRA will be providing a lunch at the NEGOP headquarters at 1610 N St. in Lincoln before the floor vote. Lunch will start at 11:30. Come join other freedom loving Americans before the vote for a bite to eat! A Caution on Spending In the coming weeks the NEGOP will be taking a look at all the new spending proposals coming from both parties. There IS merit to some new spending to solve critical problems. However, the fiscal footnotes continue to show a little here, a little there, a lot here, a lot there, and boom you have a new level of government spending. Our platform for Republicans is clear we are for lower taxes and fiscal responsibility. Lower taxes can only occur with lower spending. The Governor has been clear that he wants to scrub every dollar for efficiency. We agree with that assessment. The state is blessed with a large surplus. However, we believe that surplus belongs to the people. We are encouraging our Senators to look at the “Whole” of new spending and see where cuts can be made and ask if we really need this expenditure or if that increase to an existing program is wise. Lower spending enables lower taxes. Psychologically, we know more money in the wallet creates loose spending. We need to overcome that psychology and take a hard look at the new proposals. "Do we really need this?", should be the primary question. We need to remain diligent on spending so that we can lower tax rates and be competitive in the region for the long haul. Banking Problems The economic world was stunned when two of the largest banks collapsed. The response by Biden and the Federal Reserve has damaged the future trajectory of the banks that support Nebraska. The actions of the Federal Reserve backstopped all the deposits of customers over 250k of those large banks in large urban hubs. 250k is the amount insured by the FDIC. They elected to supersede those limits. Meanwhile, Senator Lankford of Oklahoma grilled Yellen that it was unfair that a smaller bank was not bailed out and was allowed to fail by the Federal Reserve. This action continues to show the favoritism and Inequality Under the Law practiced by our government. The decision communicated to the market that your deposits are safe at the large institutions (Class Shareholders of the Fed typically) and not safe at the smaller banks. This message being communicated is having depositors question where they will bank their deposits “To Be Safe”. A reduction of deposits to smaller banks will hurt our local institutions and economy. It is these smaller banks that are the bedrock of Nebraska communities from loans to the sponsorships of Little League Teams. It is imperative that our federal delegation restore Equality Before the Law for the sake of our local banks that make our local communities run. Big banks winning and small banks losing just show how out of touch the Fed and this President is with rural America and bedrock concepts of Equality Before the Law. Meanwhile, the Central Bank continues to push through laws in every state of the land. Controversy developed when Governor Noem put a stop to the bill in South Dakota that yielded the power of the people and currencies of their choice (not subject to the printing press) to Central Bank control. You can watch that video here. LB 214 carried by Senator Slama is the Nebraska version of the bill Governor Noem vetoed. Senator Slama promised to look into every line of that bill after the controversy took hold in Nebraska. The NEGOP supports equality before the law and a return of understanding of the Constitution as stated in Article I, Section 8, Clause 5. Monetary policy that has drifted from Constitutional understanding is having dramatic consequences. We encourage our Federal Delegation to fight for the interests of equality in all banking and the freedom of the people to choose currencies not manipulated by the printing press since the Congress has relegated its power to the Federal Reserve. Special Acknowledgment The NEGOP wishes to recognize Senator Ray Aguilar and Senator Mike Jacobsen for their votes. Senator Aguilar showed true grit and determination as he was brought into the chamber in a wheelchair with severe discomfort due to knee surgery. Even in pain, he cast a critical vote this week. In a culture where we hear complaints of how it should be made easier to vote...we saw the duty and the value of having the right to vote, by Senator Aguilar, who endured to push the button. Absolutely amazing. Senator Jacobsen faced a stiff fine for not being at an important banking governance meeting so he could be here to vote. To subject himself to a personal fine to stand for the kids of Nebraska does not go unnoticed. The sacrifices for kids and principles is inspiring. Despite the chaos the media wants you to see, we found the moments that really inspire and show our true values. We don’t see hate. We see a love of kids, and people willing to pay a price for their long term interests at the Unicameral this week. Until Next Week... Todd WatsonNEGOP Political Director political@ne.gop | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTYThis email was sent by: Nebraska Republican Party1610 N Street, Lincoln, NE, 68508 | | | | | You've received this email because you are a subscriber of . If you feel you received it by mistake or wish to unsubscribe, please .

name: me, Chuck (2)
subject: I hope this finds you well!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Grassley Committee, Inc

how are you?

Hey friends,

I hope this email finds you well.

In the new year, I'm going to fight for Iowa. But I need your help. As you know, my efforts to help retake the Senate Majority and the White House are funded by people like you, and I'm counting on your support to keep fighting for our state and our values.

**[_I'm asking you to please consider making a donation today. Whether it's $10 or $100, every contribution helps and is greatly appreciated._](http://links.grassleyworks.com/u/click?_t=65a7ce1d347445428d0d4f22dfbcf47b&_m=4c99fb4787574c5992765fff54e613be&_e=9n8Vre0LU8Ieli0usZMkFmLPlFtbgzc2ECJEExCOSnEvKIu0XChWcQwRqDtmzQM_vwRwwBVEcWveN5a-FTtynaW5vTyS-o3sWwtCA_GN8L_w9UWOvKS9kawbbD9WRJ-ySZbU-DtsPJItrTtFQ7Drrup4QHfCDsDjBR_34xrU_dfRNbso4XSVhVs2tybhOD3PlLuEs1MbpIcfOE1zyW-Locoz8GjVz3b0MvCuGrQM2eYW6a4uZSxeMILCr3hy8gNtAAwk-F8syT6fDsVl8Oyn-Bf8lFuW6LZWphknlrwSfNMKWCmIxG-sCuYVDXyue3Voc7-1hAKJT95Tf7D7uKRRKjr1v99_NIMHc6iNiEa2YTDrM4SmtNwaPsOS6CPleVq7l6cp8s3xD26l7cCATUVX0mD_5ewX3GWJbp3DqCHcyzQpWPUxysDJmiw3FhkfjoYUX_Cfdu0eCnGYZs4J5U5c00tHEFhueq7CxhHFWtOVTHGGV-4ZSHQr2lrCxS4avURA)
**
Together, we can make sure Iowa's voice is heard loud and clear in Washington!

Thank you for your support,
Chuck Grassley

_P.S. Your donation will help me continue to fight for issues that matter to Iowans like lower taxes, protecting our farmers, and lowering Rx prices!
_

| Paid for by The Grassley Committee, Inc.

| | Have you heard? We need to reform Social Security. Some of us (me and you) want to ensure the millionaires pay into the program at the same rate as everyone else. **But some Republican politicians are trying to raise the retirement age to 70. We can't let that happen.** Background: The way Social Security was set up during the New Deal era was pretty simple. Folks who are working pay a little every month out of their paychecks. But there is a cap: today, if you earn less than $160,200, you pay 6.2% of every dollar into the Social Security fund. But for every dollar over that cap you will pay exactly 0% into the fund. So, while those of us who make less than $160,200 pay 6.2% of our income to Social Security, Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan Chase, paid 0.6696% of his $1.5 million salary into Social Security. About a tenth of what we pay. **It seems pretty clear what's wrong with Social Security. The wealthy aren't paying into the program at the same rate as everyone else.** **[We are stepping up to make sure we hold the rich and powerful accountable. Can you donate now and join this movement to lift the cap on Social Security?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60784334/398173477/-641752998?refcode=2023-03-26-socsec&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=4dec5cf0-29cc-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZGVjNWNmMC0yOWNjLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-PncMKRgoeHGewsp2okgTsHqlSB-qRGlB94xQg_YYBg=)** **[Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60784335/398173478/-641752998?refcode=2023-03-26-socsec&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=4dec5cf0-29cc-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZGVjNWNmMC0yOWNjLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-PncMKRgoeHGewsp2okgTsHqlSB-qRGlB94xQg_YYBg=)** **Republicans want to raise the age of retirement to 70.** Not only are they asking for you to pay a greater portion of your income to subsidize the rich, but also to work longer while they retire early. **Our movement is about fighting for you, not CEOs.** If we are going to hold the rich and powerful accountable, we need to grow our grassroots organization today. **[Will you join our movement and help us take back all of Congress?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60784336/398173479/-641752998?refcode=2023-03-26-socsec&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=4dec5cf0-29cc-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZGVjNWNmMC0yOWNjLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-PncMKRgoeHGewsp2okgTsHqlSB-qRGlB94xQg_YYBg=)** Onward! | | We have a big announcement, friend: **Congressman Tom Kean introduced his**[**first bill**](https://link.tomkean.com/u/click?_t=9b7a451def6d4254858fc869cf07cd48&_m=2ff8d5b4ae734247bbf0c15ea5997b72&_e=qpz8kfIg-cA3xLfXVA_2VmqsFxf2ELxFnYtwB06RNCGGHJ3nvEkWfYpp6esx8xZYRZaqxTTQIlEKvJJOYWAcO9q9BrtnE-z-zhhISCnhPENEH0zRiUm7SL9Ks5tdc6tBXxea5ER-_QRZXrNIY59t1m9BXeH9tXsYDGiyJPftk0HfjKy0oRTuedE-Ci8ivWgWoRP510DQWYG8laiiLSVQMwscVNcnVE6ja4_6m032e0hw38IFQl_8BiBMWpo3A-EBmR2P2K7wgc-LpYkel-c9JakWKAyzOmDL8kljE_hCz1kHfnzXouPrLqccnBlov-CRgoX27pDZmRTU7Ru5iqoaxqSqe3r7sSW278HQiLeatdwgTIiTfrEur7viUQNBWbUa)**to the United States Congress that will address the inconvenience of multi-stop train rides for NJ commuters.**
---
[](https://link.tomkean.com/u/click?_t=9b7a451def6d4254858fc869cf07cd48&_m=2ff8d5b4ae734247bbf0c15ea5997b72&_e=qpz8kfIg-cA3xLfXVA_2VmqsFxf2ELxFnYtwB06RNCGGHJ3nvEkWfYpp6esx8xZYRZaqxTTQIlEKvJJOYWAcO9q9BrtnE-z-zhhISCnhPENEH0zRiUm7SL9Ks5tdc6tBXxea5ER-_QRZXrNIY59t1m9BXeH9tXsYDGiyJPftk0HfjKy0oRTuedE-Ci8ivWgWoRP510DQWYG8laiiLSVQMwscVNcnVE6ja4_6m032e0gAnjhNb2zQ7el-hUw3MpJ5kkAcKPHK7UOGXEujM1yg3mfLAYbQI5NRVpy4MU-NV9JkhR-GTbWFzcrXnn8XmSJi_1dIIzbIs01eROqNoq-cu9E2M3lxc2L9_16-i1DlNuf1CBZWXKZSx0ghGO_axwjo)
---
Inefficiencies in our train system have been eating up New Jersey commuters’ time for far too long. _Simply put:_**_You shouldn’t have to take multiple trains to get to New York City, and Tom is working in Congress to fix that. _** Rep. Kean’s plan would have the U.S. Department of Transportation conduct a national study of commuter lines that lack a one-seat train ride to and from major cities. This study would lay out, for the first time on a national level, what the inefficiencies and time additions are...and the implications they have on our economy. Your time is valuable, and Tom Kean knows that improving New Jersey’s transit system means less time spent commuting, less cars on the road, and more time spent at home with your families. [**> > Sign the petition to show your support for Tom’s first bill <<**](https://link.tomkean.com/u/click?_t=9b7a451def6d4254858fc869cf07cd48&_m=2ff8d5b4ae734247bbf0c15ea5997b72&_e=qpz8kfIg-cA3xLfXVA_2VmqsFxf2ELxFnYtwB06RNCGGHJ3nvEkWfYpp6esx8xZYRZaqxTTQIlEKvJJOYWAcO9q9BrtnE-z-zhhISCnhPENEH0zRiUm7SL9Ks5tdc6tBXxea5ER-_QRZXrNIY59t1m9BXeH9tXsYDGiyJPftk0HfjKy0oRTuedE-Ci8ivWgWoRP510DQWYG8laiiLSVQMwscVNcnVE6ja4_6m032e0g4JrGS9fWc0kSgz54Khyx-j2wXxrlaDz5Rwdcw4x0IX-ycdygVD6E8-XALdh6P1MbykAQU0LC26eMqmWy1JEQWMU_BIxb-rKvwbx9kA5Y-dNi4y7AQpYzo0N4dSj7leeOzm3rK5IKrZ6tLDpJzoIG9) Thank you,Team Kean

Let us explain why **[your $5 contribution today](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60784523/398174252/1070860144?amount=5&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20230326_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDIxYTBlOS0yZmNjLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-oJ_6f3jDZ29DU1pH3tDaLwg5jUb07GoBYsSwFNWuYE=)** would make a huge difference to our campaign, especially as we’re 5 days away from our first end-of-quarter deadline of the year. If you knew that your $5 donation would help us defend Martin’s Senate seat in New Mexico and the Democratic majority along with it, wouldn’t you consider it a worthwhile investment? With the help of this grassroots team, we made history last November – flipping four chambers and holding onto every single majority. **But we can’t stop now.**
We’re already preparing for our next big fight – state Democrats have a slew of special elections in the next two months, and the Virginia House and Senate are in play this November. President Obama is calling out the importance of Democratic leadership in the states – it’s up to grassroots supporters like you to listen to him and help elect state Democrats now. **By renewing your 2023 DLCC membership _today_ you can help pave a path for Democratic wins this year and beyond. **
**As an official member, you’ll be at the forefront of the fight against state Republicans’ extremist agenda, helping elect Democrats who will work to secure a progressive future for states like yours. So, can we count you in?**
